themes.label: Nuclear / AI Power Infrastructure
themes.narrative: Nuclear SMR permitting news (5 headline hits, HIGH signal) and 'AI data center power demand crisis' coverage (utility ETFs +8% YTD) have the market treating power infrastructure as a single overheated trade.
themes.dataAnchor: The revenue-confirmed suppliers are still deeply dislocated: BWXT is -22.7% from its 52wH with revenue +26.1% YoY and positive FCF ($185M); NXT is -30.9% from high with revenue +31.6% YoY, FULL_SIGNAL on the scanner. Both clear all three triple-alignment floors (macro/quality/momentum).
themes.counterRead: The narrative and the dislocation depth are pointing opposite directions for the same theme. Capital has already started separating winners from story stocks: speculative pre-revenue nuclear-adjacent names (SMR -15.5%, OKLO -16.4% over 1 month) are being sold hard while the revenue-backed suppliers (BWXT, NXT) sit at a trough. That is the setup, not a reason for caution.
themes.watchFor: BWXT (Aug 2026) and NXT earnings confirming the revenue-recovery trend continues without margin slippage.

themes.label: Index-Level Calm vs. Stock-Level Dispersion
themes.narrative: VIX collapsing to 15.84 (-6.3% today, -16.3% over 1 month) alongside SPY/QQQ/IWM up 14-23% over 3 months reads as 'the market is fine, risk is off the table.'
themes.dataAnchor: Underneath that calm, a long list of individual names remain 20-64% below their 52-week highs with active sentiment-driven signals: PLTR -37.8%, APP -30.2%, CLS -25.2%, HUBS -63.8%, KTOS -63.5%, NOW -48.2%. Credit is tight but not loose (HY OAS 2.70, real 10Y 2.33%) — there is little room for a shock to be absorbed painlessly.
themes.counterRead: Index-level vol compression is masking real stock-specific carnage in growth/software/defense names. Low VIX does not mean low idiosyncratic risk right now — it means the index is being carried by a narrow set of leaders (MA, META, GEV, VRT) while dispersion underneath stays wide. Treat index calm as a backdrop, not a signal that individual names have de-risked.
themes.watchFor: HY OAS breaking above ~3.0, or VIX reversing back above ~20 — either would flag the calm as complacency rather than confirmation.

themes.label: Enterprise / AI Software SaaS Dislocation
themes.narrative: A year of 'AI will commoditize SaaS' fear has priced enterprise software as if growth is over: PLTR -37.8% from high, APP -30.2%, HUBS -63.8%, NOW -48.2%.
themes.dataAnchor: The revenue math says otherwise for the strongest names. PLTR: revenue +56.2% YoY, FCF positive, quality_math 40/40 — TRIPLE_ALIGNED at a 92 composite score, the top-ranked name in the current scan. APP: revenue +24.2% YoY, 76% gross margin, FCF margin 60.8% — but still failing the macro_fit floor (8/30), a genuine near-miss, not yet actionable.
themes.counterRead: For names that clear the macro floor (PLTR, CLS, HUBB) the market is pricing a growth deceleration that the reported numbers do not support — an actual narrative/data gap, not just a beaten-down chart. For APP (and GOOGL, KNSL), the quality math is equally strong but the regime/macro fit for their specific theme isn't there yet, which is the difference between 'cheap' and 'actionable.'
themes.watchFor: APP, GOOGL, or KNSL clearing the macro_fit floor on the next scan run — would promote them from near-miss to aligned.

themes.label: Speculative Nuclear-Adjacent & Space Unwind
themes.narrative: The same nuclear/space/AI-infrastructure story that lifted GEV (+15.2% 1m) and VRT (+8.0% 1m) was, until recently, also being applied to pre-revenue or early-stage names in the same theme cluster.
themes.dataAnchor: SMR -15.5%, OKLO -16.4%, RKLB -27.0%, and ASTS -19.7% over the past month — all falling hard even as the broader index and the moat-holding leaders in the same theme continue higher. This is a clean divergence within one narrative bucket.
themes.counterRead: This is the narrative unwinding exactly where it should: capital rotating out of story-only exposure and into names with confirmed revenue and moat (BWXT, NXT, GEV, VRT). It's a healthy sign for the theme's investable names, not a reason to distrust the theme itself.
themes.watchFor: SMR/OKLO/RKLB stabilizing vs. continuing to fall — confirms whether this is rotation-out-of-speculation (bullish for BWXT/NXT/GEV) or the start of broader risk-off in small-cap growth.

strategyPosture: Index-level risk-on is real (SPY/QQQ/IWM up 14-23% over 3 months, VIX crushed to 15.84) but it is papering over sharp stock-specific dispersion — several names with strong, verified revenue math (PLTR, NXT, BWXT, AVGO) remain 20-64% below their highs. Lean into the triple-aligned, evidence-backed list rather than chasing index strength, and keep entries disciplined given tight credit (HY OAS 2.70) leaves little room to absorb a shock.
whereWaveBuilds: The next move is most likely a broadening of the rotation already visible in AI power/nuclear (out of speculative SMR/OKLO/RKLB, into revenue-confirmed BWXT/NXT) into the still-dislocated AI-adjacent software names (PLTR, CLS) that have cleared the same quality and momentum bar but remain priced for a growth slowdown the numbers don't show.
watchFor: HY OAS (2.70) breaking above ~3.0 — the tight-credit assumption underpinning the current risk-on regime
VIX (15.84, -16.3% 1m) reversing back above ~20 — would reframe the current calm as complacency
BWXT (Aug 2026) and AVGO (Sep 2026) earnings confirming the revenue-recovery thesis behind the conviction promotions
SMR/OKLO/RKLB stabilizing vs. continuing to fall — signals whether AI-power capital rotation or broader small-cap risk-off is underway
APP, GOOGL, or KNSL clearing the macro_fit floor — would move them from near-miss to an actionable aligned candidate
BMNR (-12.5% 1m) continuing to diverge from ETH (+2.1% 1m) — would confirm BMNR-specific weakness rather than crypto-beta
